Title: Ecotype-specific phenolic acid accumulation and root softness in Salvia miltiorrhiza are driven by environmental and genetic factors

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Rosmarinic acid branch; also linked to caffeoylquinic acid/S-type lignin precursor-related branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Caffeoyl-CoA + danshensu; alternatively caffeoyl-CoA + quinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Rosmarinic acid; alternatively caffeoyl quinic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a caffeoyl group from caffeoyl-CoA to danshensu to form rosmarinic acid; can also acylate quinic acid with caffeoyl-CoA to produce caffeoyl quinic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and also diverts flux toward competing lignin-related precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SmRAS146C promotes rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid B accumulation and hairy root growth, whereas SmRAS146G favors caffeoyl quinic acid accumulation, thicker cell walls, and stronger lignification-related traits
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SmRAS is a central clustered enzyme gene with two major haplotypes, 146C in Sm.SC and 146G in Sm.SD. 2. In vitro assays showed SmRAS catalyzes rosmarinic acid formation from caffeoyl-CoA and danshensu, and also catalyzes caffeoyl quinic acid formation from caffeoyl-CoA and quinic acid. 3. The 146C haplotype shows higher affinity for RA-pathway substrates and is associated with higher RA and SAB in populations. 4. Hairy-root overexpression of SmRAS146C increased root growth and RA accumulation, while SmRAS146G shifted flux toward caffeoylquinic acid and cell-wall thickening. 5. In Arabidopsis athct2 mutants, SmRAS haplotypes rescued lignin/cell-wall defects, with 146G showing stronger compensation for lignin-related traits.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in Sm.SC than in Sm.SD mature roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in Sm.SC than in Sm.SD mature roots
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal compounds associated with stress adaptation and quality traits in Danshen
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ecotype background; UV-B; low temperature; Cu stress; heterologous expression contexts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SmRAS overexpression in Arabidopsis Col-0 inhibited growth and development; 146G haplotype favored lignification/cell wall thickening over RA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Population haplotype association; subcellular localization; recombinant protein expression; in vitro enzyme assay; kinetic analysis; molecular docking; hairy-root overexpression; metabolite profiling by HPLC; heterologous complementation in Arabidopsis athct2
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10 promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; UBQ-GFP control
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Select or introgress the 146C haplotype to increase RA/SAB and softer-root medicinal quality; deploy 146G when stronger lignification/cell-wall traits are desired; haplotype-informed breeding is recommended
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; resequencing; RNA-seq; metabolomics; DAP-seq (study states deposited in NCBI and National Genomics Data Center, accession not provided)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RAS is described as Lamiaceae-specific in the cluster and retains ancestral acyltransferase activity typical of BAHD enzymes; the paper links RAS variation to metabolic divergence by a single SNP causing an amino-acid substitution and structural change

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor family; N-terminal WRKY domain
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SmTAT2; SmHPPR; SmRAS; SmCYP98A14; SmCCR/SmCCR2; SmHCT (binding retained in Sm.SD but absent in Sm.SC)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of SmRAS, SmCYP98A14, SmCCR2 and binds promoters of SmTAT2, SmHPPR, SmRAS, SmCYP98A14 and SmCCR; ecotype-specific lack of binding to SmHCT promoter in Sm.SC
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenolic acid biosynthesis; tyrosine metabolism; lignin biosynthesis; cell wall thickening/xylem development
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA; yeast extract; UV-B; low temperature; NaCl; drought
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SmWRKY40 is essential for promoting rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid B accumulation, lignin deposition, xylem development, root hardness, and stress adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key regulator/essential activator at the center of a phenolic-acid metabolic gene cluster; strong association with RA, SAB and S-type lignin components
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Comparative genomics and integrative omics identified SmWRKY40 at the core of a phenolic acid/lignin gene cluster unique to the Sichuan ecotype. 2. SmWRKY40 is highly expressed in roots and induced by ABA, yeast extract, UV-B, cold, salt and drought. 3. Overexpression in S. miltiorrhiza hairy roots increased RA, SAB, lignin content, xylem development and root growth, while CRISPR knockout reduced growth and caused softer roots. 4. In Arabidopsis, SmWRKY40 promoted growth and lignin biosynthesis and complemented the atwrky40 mutant. 5. DAP-seq plus Y1H, dual-LUC and EMSA demonstrated direct W-box binding to multiple phenolic-acid and lignin pathway genes, with ecotype-specific target divergence, especially at SmHCT.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots of both Sm.SC and Sm.SD; higher in Sm.SC than Sm.SD inferred from cluster expression context
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; induced by ABA, yeast extract, UV-B, low temperature, NaCl and drought
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major medicinal bioactive compounds; associated with stress adaptation and quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Lower temperature and increased UV-B promote RA/SAB accumulation; Cu stress reduces them; SmWRKY40 is stress responsive under UV-B, cold, NaCl, drought, ABA and yeast extract
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: CRISPR knockout reduced root growth and softened roots; overexpression increased sensitivity to Cu2+ stress while improving UV-B and cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression/integrated omics network analysis; subcellular localization; transcriptional activation assay; hairy-root overexpression and CRISPR knockout; HPLC metabolite profiling; histology/SEM; qPCR of targets; transgenic/complementation in Arabidopsis; DAP-seq; dual-LUC; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering high-RA/SAB and quality-improved Danshen; suitable for marker-assisted selection or transgenic/editing strategies, but Cu sensitivity and lignification effects should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; comparative genomics; resequencing; RNA-seq; metabolomics; DAP-seq (study states deposited in NCBI and National Genomics Data Center, accession not provided)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SmWRKY40 is highly conserved compared with neighboring cluster genes and appears to mediate ecotype-specific regulatory rewiring; in Sm.SC loss of SmHCT promoter binding may contribute to altered S-type lignin pattern
